Todoist vs Fireflies.ai: Which Is Better?

Choosing between Todoist and Fireflies.ai? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose Todoist if you need freelancers or solo-devs. Choose Fireflies.ai if you prioritize remote-teams or sales-teams.

Todoist

PricingFree / $4/mo Pro / $6/mo Business
Rating★★★★ 4.4/5

Key Features

  • Natural language parsing for instant task creation
  • Labels, filters, and custom views for flexible task organization
  • Cross-platform apps (Mac, Windows, iOS, Android, web, wearables)
  • Project templates and shared projects for recurring workflows
  • Productivity tracker and goal-setting with progress visualization
  • Integrations with Zapier, IFTTT, Google Calendar, and 60+ other tools

Pros

  • Natural language input is the gold standard — 'pay rent on the 1st every month at 9am' parses perfectly every time
  • Fast doesn't begin to describe it; the app opens instantly on every platform and sync is near-instant
  • Labels, filters, and custom views let you slice your tasks however you want (by energy level, context, project)
  • Karma gamification is subtle and actually motivating without being annoying
  • The collaboration features are simple and effective for shared shopping lists, small team tasks, or family chores

Cons

  • Not built for complex project management — no Gantt charts, no dependencies, no resource management
  • Collaboration is basic; you can share projects but don't expect granular permissions or review workflows
  • The free tier is good but limits you to 5 active projects and some features are gated behind Pro
  • No real file management — you can attach files, but there's no organized file storage per project
  • Boards/kanban view was added late and feels bolted on compared to native kanban tools
Try Todoist →

Fireflies.ai

PricingFree / $10/mo Pro / $19/mo Business
Rating★★★★ 4.2/5

Key Features

  • Automatic meeting recording and transcription across Zoom, Meet, Teams
  • AI-powered meeting summaries with key decisions and action items
  • Searchable meeting history across your entire team
  • Soundbites for clipping and sharing specific moments from recordings
  • Conversation intelligence for sales teams and deal analysis
  • Integrations with Notion, Slack, Asana, Salesforce, and HubSpot

Pros

  • Auto-joins meetings and records them without anyone needing to remember or click anything
  • Transcription accuracy is good for clean audio, though it struggles with heavy accents or overlapping speech
  • AI summaries capture key decisions, action items, and questions in a digestible format
  • Search works across your entire meeting history — type 'budget decision' and find the exact conversation
  • Integrates with Slack, Notion, Salesforce, and Asana for pushing notes into your workflow automatically

Cons

  • Free tier is limited to 3 meetings total — not enough for a proper evaluation
  • Transcription accuracy varies significantly with background noise, speaker overlap, or accents
  • Privacy concerns about having an AI bot in every meeting can unsettle team members or clients
  • Action item detection is useful but not reliable enough to trust completely; you'll still need to verify
  • The web app can be slow with large meeting histories; searching across 500+ recordings lags
Try Fireflies.ai →

Which Should You Choose?

Both Todoist and Fireflies.ai are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose Todoist if: Todoist is the rare productivity tool that's both powerful enough for GTD nerds and simple enough for people who just want to stop forgetting things. The magic is the natural language input — type 'buy groceries every Saturday at 10am' and it just works. It's been around for 15 years and somehow still feels modern.
  • Choose Fireflies.ai if: Fireflies is the AI meeting assistant that joins your calls, records everything, transcribes the conversation, and generates summaries. It works with Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, and Webex — and it's gotten good enough that you'll stop taking notes entirely. The searchable history is the killer feature: you can find any decision, commitment, or name mentioned across hundreds of past meetings.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
